revision_summary: Three targeted fixes required before this output is production-ready: (1) INTENT LABEL INCONSISTENCY — rep_intent.summary must be changed from 'reactive' to 'indeterminate' or 'unknown.' The trajectory_verdict correctly declares the call unanalyzable; assigning a directional intent label in the same output contradicts that conclusion and could mislead a manager reading only summary fields. (2) ADDRESS SEQUENCE CHERRY PICK — the 00:29–00:36 address sequence ('Then go to direction / West / Market / in Chicago, Illinois') is the most coherent multi-fragment sequence in the transcript and should be promoted from grounded_examples to cherry_pick_moments as a possible verify.address_homeowner_confirmed beat, with the strategic read (protocol-compliant address confirmation) foregrounded rather than dismissed as 'reactive.' (3) 'SINGLES' AMBIGUITY — cherry_pick_moments[2] must acknowledge that 'Singles' at 02:15/02:21 is ambiguous between roof type (verify.roof_type_age) and household/title status (verify.title_co_owners). Stage 3 presents only the roof interpretation; both are equally plausible from the fragment alone. The codex_reference should reflect this ambiguity. Secondary fix (lower priority): the bill_collection.stewart_automated_bill_capture codex citation at cherry_pick_moments[3] is a stretch — the roadmap anti-pattern detection rule should not be cited against an uninterpretable transcription artifact. Replace with a general bill_collection reference or remove the codex_reference entirely.
